channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. — 87 replies — 348.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off.

Open thread • Replies: 87 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:35 UTC

OP

Some 16 year old told me he had worked a full week nonstop, so he was told he had to take the next five days off. I told him that was good.

I told him that most people will not make it in today’s economy when $150,000 is considered middle class. He replied that $150,000 is a lot of money. I said sure, but the average American salary is around $65,000.

Then I asked him what kind of work he did, and he said he did some low level blue collar welding. I told him blue collar work is terrible. He responded that welders can make over $100,000 a year.

I told him that blue collar work is for low-IQ, uneducated people and that the adults in those jobs are losers and people who messed up their lives. I also said blue collar work is fun when you’re a kid, but the adults doing it are there because they’re losers.

It turned out that three blue collar workers had been listening to our conversation and told the kid not to listen to me because I didn’t know what I was talking about.

I then told the kid that when he grows up, he will realize people look down on blue collar workers for a reason and that blue collar adults are not the kind of people he should want to know.

The blue collar workers called me ignorant, yet, from my perspective, they seemed to have chips on their shoulders and came across as unintelligent.

Why are blue- collar workers like this? Are they aware of how the world perceives them?

I wanted to talk numbers because no way most blue collar makes over $100k. Can someone fill me in?

Early replies (first 3)

62342722
>>62342700
Fuck off

62342735
>>62342700
I like this spam, because it counters the AI brainwashing (trades good, factory good, college bad, IT arrogant & overpaid or Indians, close the borders).

62342744
>>62342735
What?
>>62342722
Fuck you.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62349408
>>62342798
bro you're onto something.
Fake accounts FLOOD the internet. Especially kinky shit like fetlife and the kink subreddits - they will have the exact same contact for totally different account and profiles.
I found 2 accounts that had each other as friends.. they had similar profiles, said they "knew each other online" and 1 used a keyword that the other one was talking to me and using..
there is a MASSIVE psyop happening.

62349422
>>62342700
OP, you clearly haven't heard this enough, you're a faggot.

62351731
>>62346960
everything that you post (on an almost daily basis) that never actually happened outside your autistic mongoloid mind, champ. I love these stories about the "middle class" lol. Keep up the good work soldier. thanks for the laughs (unironically speaking)

62354799
>>62342905
Elliot only drove a BMW because his dad had some cushy Hollywood job working on films like Hunger Games, etc. Pretty sure he wrote something in his manifesto seething about seeing some guy driving a shitbox and still having a beautiful girl in the passenger seat too.

62354802
>>62342834
ha, i was thinking this read exactly like that post

62354821
>>62342828
When they're writing long form fiction they get a pass.

62355262
>>62342700
Electrical contractor here. I live closer to the beach than you do. It’s kind of funny, most skilled tradesmen look down on people like yourself. We feel sorry for you because in the grand scheme of things - you’re worthless. Unable to build or fix anything. Totally dependent on others. Kinda pathetic

62355284
>>62355262
The mind of a blue collar is truly pathetic.

62355301
>>62342770
I haven’t worked in 1,5 months and i can already feel myself wanting to just become passive and not really being impressed or motivated by anything (brain getting fried by dopamine and serotonine). You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.

62355307
>>62355301
You’re pathetic. Since a little kid, all the classmates of my have been retired.

62355313
>>62355301
> You have to be exactly the kind of ultra degenerate basement dweller who spends all his time in front of screens if you think that not working at all is good. Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
You’re probably just poor. People making a top 10% income from investments tend not to work.

62355322
>>62355301
>1.5 months
You don’t even have a non working lifestyle built yet.

62355326
>>62355307
This may be the funniest claim I've seen you make. Absolutely nobody from your school does any kind of work at all? Most people do find things to keep themselves occupied with, beyond mere shitposting on an anonymous uzbekistani finger painting notification board.

62355338
>>62355326
Maybe we grew up in different classes.
On one hand, /biz/ loves to larp as rich and successful and on the other hand, loves to larp as muh productive and I don’t like doing nothing.
Fucking jokes.
I hear people say what you say all the time. Yet look at retired people, I don’t see you saying shit to them. Yet when it is someone young and not working, suddenly they are “unproductive.” Even though these people are clearly rich.

62355342
>>62355307
>>62355313
>>62355322
Kek did i trigger you too much?

62355360
>>62355342
I have never in my life looked at someone not working and thought anything bad.
Growing up, my dad retired when I was like 5. By 10, the parents of all my school friends were retired long ago.
By past college, the differences between the haves and have nots became noticeable because college is where you truly see class differences because high school and under tend to mainly kids from your neighborhood.
You cannot imagine life without work yet kids that grew up like me were surrounded by adults that don’t work. It is the normal. People like me just carry on the lifestyle.
People from successful families don’t break their back.

62355375
>>62355301
Depends what you mean by work. But any occupation today is not a good use of time. Like work as in what? Something you need to get a college degree for? White collar?
Or something blue collar? That’s even dumber.
Starting your own business? Okay sure, if you want to start it as a hobby..?
I don’t know. People build lifestyles when they don’t need to work to live.
Some travel, some have many social gatherings
>Humans need the struggle in order to feel alive.
Oh yeah, the bottom 90% definitely feel alive struggling lol.
Struggling for what? Lmao.

62355404
>>62355301
Sometimes I see the kids in my neighborhood. This isnt an ultra rich 100m a year neighborhood or millions a month neighborhood but in 10-20 years when these kids grow up, it would be a waste of time for them to get a “job.”
Get it?

62357658
>>62355301
Rabbis rabbiposting as usual. Nice try but no, i won't work for (((you)))

62359024
>>62342770
I have 300k...where should i buy a house and get on gibbs? Caliwali?

62359462
>>62342700
I mean someone has to like do stuff u need someone to actually do stuff

62359491
>>62342770
This is called capitalism btw. You either exploit others or u work until you die. Hello knock knock HELLO this is basic capitalism. Wake up everything u guys complain about is due to market forces the Jews are a fantasy of cohesion to keep your little world simple bigga

62360989
Never worked a day in my life and I would never shit on a trade they keep my water running and the electricity flowing. Would you trust a 3rd worlder to wire up your outlet in your house and not expect to die in your sleep when that shit catches fire fuck no I'm going to pay a white American 150$ an hour to do that shit right. Without their goods and services our money doesn't mean shit and I might aswell get raped by a pack of niggers.

62361159
All work is automateable. No one of you should feel safe.

62361494
>>62344611
>the producing members
The secret is these types wouldnt know what to do in life other than work. Their life centers around it. You're supposed to exploit this in them

62363064
>>62361159
What about work on new products that have never been fully before
Someone has to build it first for a robot to even try

62363066
>>62360989
Based

62363074
>>62342905
BMW stock is worth about $25 kek

62363190
>>62361494
Yeah.
Half of Americans own less than 200k in networth but these people try to call me a loser cause I don’t need work.

62363514
>>62342700
The funniest part of this larp spam where you autistically have back and forth conversations about class and wealth with normies is that even in your fantasies you hold a captive audience of blue collar workers and poor office wagies, meaning that whatever your position in life, you're really not that far above them.

item.title: Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money? — 60 replies — 240.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why's he a trillionaire when his companies barely make money?

Open thread • Replies: 60 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:30 UTC

OP

>Tesla makes $3.9B/year, and the amount goes down year after year, yet has a 1.5T market cap
>SpaceX literally loses money and its claim to future profit is Grok, a cash-burning AI model that is losing to GPT and Claude
>Twitter is breakeven at best
What's going on here? Are white boomers just throwing money at him because they don't like seeing Indians, Chinese and Jews running shit in tech? This is the only explanation for the Elon Musk phenomenon I can make any sense of.

Early replies (first 3)

62361459
>>62361452
People rightly see him as a visionary for the future of humanity with the ability to actually do the stuf he foresees.

on the negative side much of what he envisions is distopian

and the first person to pioneer a technology is often NOT the person who gets rich off it

but you have to start somewhere, and he certainly does stuff other companies can't.

62361461
companies do not have to be profitable for people to speculate on them performing well in the future, and it helps when everyone said every company would go to zero but instead became some of the most valuable assets by marketcap in the world because they provided goods and services people wanted

62361471
>>62361459
>rightly
He hasn't even done anything, period.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362436
>>62362428
I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.

62362441
>>62362436
>I mean, if you had big ass space craft and thousands of engineers and a plan, maybe yea you could get $10T. But you don't.
He doesn't have enough of that stuff to get a man to mars ether, yet still he gets a trillion for owning the least profitable large cap tech stocks in America.

62362449
>>62362441
you can read the OP in my thread here: >>62362383
spaceX in their own documents aren't saying they believe in space exploration
that's just the story they are selling the the goys and retail investors

spaceX plans to make its money on building data centers, just like anthropic, oracle, openai, google, literally every other tech company right now
and again, to be clear, I agree with you this is stupid and this bubble will eventually pop. or maybe it won't. the dotcom bubble never really popped, the market just kept growing and software really was that valuable.

anyways. the stories Musk tells about space exploration are mainly marketing to get dumb people and X followers excited, he has already realized he can't deliver on that goal. the big picture for spaceX doesn't involve space exploration at all.

62362464
>>62362449
>the dotcom bubble never really popped
Get a load of this baggie.

62362537
>>62361452
Go back to rebbit, faggot.

62362557
>>62361478
>He
He doesn't do shit in his companies

62362583
He's ran a very good public relations campaign the last few decades. He made sure that his name was associated with his products. Tesla is synonymous with Elon Musk

62362589
>>62361452
Guy I'm stuck in e13 figure hell and people are making fun of me online. What do?

62362674
>>62362449
maybe the data center angle is just what they are selling to the institutional investors, and they really do believe in space exploration?

62362684
>>62361478
>robotics
he hasn't sold a single robot

62362688
>>62362068
he received massive subsidies the entire time in tesla
That's not his doing. Politicians wanted EVs no matter if he produced them or someone else did
They were already was creating a car before he joined Tesla

62362689
>>62361452
Of all the famous billionaires, Musk's companies are the only truly innovative ones. Like many of us, I don't like his liberalism or his transhumanism, but it's undeniable that the "hype" that fuels his investments is based on the significant chance that he can pull of what he says. Afterall everyone wrote off starlink and autonomous cars.

I do find it irritating how people can't talk about him or spacex in good faith. They hide behind investment language they don't understand when the truth is that they hate him and his companies because the media told them to. The regime media has had it in for him ever since he bought twitter since they had a near total monopoly on information before that point. You're seeing just how much they hate the situation by the fact that the UK grooming gang discussion in parliament was hijacked by deranged leftists into a 2 minute hate against Musk.
These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example, a truly hideous "person" who has done nothing except harm humanity and steal money.

62362700
>>62361452
Now that he's a trilly, do you goys think this bitch will still settle for that $500k/year he was giving her or will that 10x soon?

62362803
>>62362689
>they hate him and his companies because the media told them to
I simply hate all billionaires because it's fucked

62362809
>>62362689
>The regime media
the ones who pushed EVs?
>These same people never have a bad word to say about George Soros for example
you mean the currency manipulator who "broke the bank of england" ?
who did he steal money from?

62362814
>>62361452
In a sea of grifting the guy offers at least a unique perspective on his multiple scams. For that he is awarded 1 trillion dollars

62362818
>>62361452
ask yourself which space sector company can dislodge SpaceX from government contract welfare. they are now too embedded, much like Microsoft with their shitty products, and will always recieve enough taxpayer gibs to keep them profitable while also paying investors. literally nobody else can compete

62362863
>>62361452
It's a mystery

62362883
>>62361478
He makes promises he can't keep and retards like you fall for it every time which keep the stocks inflated.

62362985
>>62361452
It's paper networth. His net worth is in stock shares. The stock market has nothing to do with real life and is just a popularity contest. Buying a share of Tesla and thinking you own a piece of Tesla despite it never paying a dividend or doing a buyback is no different than buying dodgecoin and thinking u own a stake in the dog. His stocks only value is just what ever u can tell to for to the next person. All he does is pull from science fiction and that keeps his stock popular. It's literally no different from a casino. Ultimately this gambling money comes from the petro dollar

62363104
>>62362557
Poorfag doesn't bussines

62363313
>>62361452
People are so impressed by the self-landing rockets and the sci fi visions that they throw their money at him without any thought on whether the numbers are reasonable.

62363342
>>62362818
US government contracts are only 21% of SpaceX revenue ("Customer A" in the prospectus), and the growth potential is limited.

There is no strong vendor lock-in with SpaceX like there is with Microsoft. The US government absolutely wants to diversify its vendor base and will give contracts to other vendors at earliest opportunity

62363359
>>62361966
she also has an unlimited customer base

62363375
>>62361478
>EVs and robots
requires the us government to ban chinese evs and robots because they're raping him in price lmao, if elon is worth 1 trillion cuz muh robots then unitree is worth 100 trillion lmao

62363382
>>62361478
He's not ahead of the competition in anything except space.

For a time, he was ahead in EVs. This he achieved by leveraging advancements in battery technology by Japanese and Korean companies, while other car companies were hesitant because they did not want to cannibalize their existing ICE product lines.

62363405
>>62361452
everything is fake and gay in this economy and World retard
>SpaceX's valuation is expected to be nearly $1.8 trillion, revenue was 18 billion in 2025
>Toyota valuation is at 230 billion, revenue in 2025 was 320 billion

62363434
>>62363405
we don't need more cars
we need more spaceships
simple as

62363442
>>62361499
I'll believe sodium batteries when i see them. The voltage curve of the ones i've seen so far are horrible--a car at 100% charge might be fast but at 50% charge might be dog slow.

62363501
>>62363442
No no everything that comes out of China is bleeding edge and perfect on the first try.

item.title: No regrets selling BTC?? — 94 replies — 138.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
No regrets selling BTC??

Open thread • Replies: 94 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:01 UTC

OP

Some talk about regretting it.

Anyone sold and still thinks it was the right move?

If you had the cash from that sale sitting in your account today, would you buy BTC again? Because I definitely would.

Early replies (first 3)

62339349
I sold my last bitcoin last year. Shitcoin is dead. AI is dead. Time to start acting like an african american.

62339372
>>62339349
Maybe regrets for buying? Should have bought chainlink

62339382
I rotated into XMR at like $105k, no regrets at all.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62352286
>>62350315
Ethereum has certainly built one of the strongest ecosystems in crypto.

62354098
>>62351129
You have a point there

62354803
>>62350283
Maybe this is how people finally start exploring BTC in DeFi. Participation is still tiny. Imagine what happens if even 1% starts becoming 2%, then 5%.

62354820
>>62351106
I've actually earned more from memecoins than utility coins lately lol.

62354827
>>62339328
Anyone bullish on avalanche?

62355050
>>62339328
I sold when the Epstein files dropped. Feels good.

62355133
>>62354803
That’s the part a lot of people overlook. If TBVs actually work as intended, I think people are underestimating how much TVL native BTC use cases could pull in.

62355136
>>62355050
Time will tell whether it was the right call, but at least you acted according to your move.

62355617
>>62355133
It's crazy that DeFi security is still one of the biggest problems in the space.

62355631
>>62354827
Buy avax today and sell @ $100

62355632
>>62339417
Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
And monero is based on bitcoin. The entire crypto market exists because of all the money in bitcoin which is now backed by the US government.

62356269
>>62355617
At this point, thorough audits and multiple layers of review should be the standard, not a nice bonus anon.

62356538
>>62355631
What's your basis?

62357046
>>62345600
Probably the better route. We've seen too many centralized services blow up over the years, so you can't really blame people for preferring fully decentralized setups.

62357057
>>62355632
What about ZEC?

62357077
ICP

62357115
>>62339328
I sold at 116k I dont regret it at all and have about 2 mil waiting for sept-dec buy in.

62357126
>>62339328
I can never regret buying BTC desu, I bought back in around 70K and will DCA more if price goes below 60K. I'm also staking a little BTC on babylon for some extra yield

62357923
>>62355632
>Monero programmers, like the telegram founder, can be compromised
yeah just like the bitcoin developers right? they're the ones who control bitcoin?

it's a good question though, what if they were? miners would have to agree on consensus on which code to accept. if github ever refused to host monero's code then it would just move to .onion or .i2p

62359525
>>62357046
That's how it goes. They've already got GoMining, Ledger, and a16z involved, so let's see who they bring in next.

62359541
>>62357057
ZEC is interesting. If the privacy narrative picks up again, it's usually one of the first names people bring up.

62359557
>>62339328
I literally don't care about it. Mogged by virtually everything else over the last couple of years.

Also why is faggot shit like dogecoin still worth billions of dollars.

>in b4 bottom signal
Probably.

62359588
>>62357126
Sounds like you've got a plan and you're sticking to it. Hard to ask for much more than that. I wouldn't be surprised if more people follow suit over time. The $4B+ TVL shows there's already plenty of interest.

62359608
>>62359557
The market has a weird habit of rewarding the things nobody can rationally explain.

62359673
>>62357077
People still remember the launch, but the tech discussion around ICP is often more interesting than the price chart.

62360479
>>62359557
When bottom

62361782
>>62360479
Nobody knows

62362657
>>62359557
How do you mean?

62362661
>>62339328
While BTC is in deep bear stage, it's time to get in. I need to be greedy when others are fear.

62363445
>>62362657
It's his opinion

item.title: Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun — 22 replies — 51.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
Elon is going to build a data center dAIson sphere around the sun

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:16 UTC

OP

Buy space exploration technologies company stock BUY BUY BUY

Early replies (first 3)

62359811
>>62359800
kek

62359825
>>62359800
will that thing cast a shadow on my backyard? my petunias need the sunlight

62359829
>>62359800
This may be controversial but I think we will see a Dyson Sphere within our lives.

Technology is exponential. Look how long we spent in the Dark Ages with basic mathematics and science then we hit the industrial revolution and in a few hundred of years we are here.

Now with the AI revolution things will happen even faster. Technology enables us to do things faster, better, and more efficiently. With AI we will be travelling between planets regularly in just a few decades.

It might sound crazy but so would a smart phone 200 years ago.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62359836
>>62359829
I hope not, it's terribly ugly. Futurists really don't seem to have any aesthetic sense whatsoever. What point is there in traveling to other planets if the people going there are hideous and uglify everything they touch?

62359837
When is Elon going to build a Mass Relay to the Citadel? I need some blue Asari ass

62359841
>space exploration technologies

62359842
>>62359829
Unless we get real Artificial Superintelligence soon you can forget about that.

62360179
>>62359841
inaneh compootah

62360249
>>62359800
Yes.

62360253
EDS trannies will literally say this is fake, but it's real and it's happening. 10,000 Starship launches per year by 2028, the dyson sphere is happening

62360297
>>62359800
kek

62360306
>>62359800
By 2027 none the less!!!!

62360337
They will build special space radiators of carbon and shit and lasers

62360362
>>62359800
Who's gonna build it? Mexicans?

62360396
>>62359836
its not about beauty, its about turning energy into compute

62360433
>>62359836
How often do you look at the sun

62360446
>>62360362
AI ROBOTS

62360616
>>62359829
Absolutely no chance. A Dyson sphere isn't a single monolithic structure, it's a series of maybe trillions of separate space habitats, some connected together as islands and archipelagos, and many (but not all) spinning for artificial gravity.
It isn't built as one big project, it grows organically as the population it sustains increases. A Dyson sphere can support somewhere in the realm of 50 quintillion people, and our population increases roughly 1-3% per year. Even if you account for people intentionally reproducing much more frequently and living longer, the need for a full Dyson sphere is still a really, really long way away.

I think it is plausible but unlikely that we could build a rotating space habitat (O'Neill cylinder) in an L4 or L5 solar orbit in our lifetimes. But it will largely come down to real estate prices. Once it's cheaper to build in space than to buy land on Earth, we'll start doing it. There are a lot of key technology steps we're waiting on to make it affordable. Mining the asteroid belt is a big one, but I argue that the biggest problem is launch costs. Once we have a more efficient way to get things into space, the cost of building up there drops enormously. Probably the most feasible option right now is a tethered ring space launcher like from Project Atlantis, but it still has a lot of problems. One day we will likely have a series of orbital rings around the Earth which make getting up into orbit as easy and cheap as catching a train.

But don't misunderstand me, the future of humanity is absolutely in space. It's almost certain that one day the supermajority of humanity will live in rotating space habitats rather than on planets.

62363032
>>62359800
He's trying to get to mars which is much closer and in the opposite direction. A dyson sphere the size of the sun would not be possible there isn't enough material available on our planet to construct even half of a ring.

62363034
>>62359825
We'll build it just beyond Mars' orbit, so only outer planets shitters will be caught in the shade

62363039
The solar datacenter could power everything we need. Youtube, facebook, and x. It's literally free.

62363476
>>62360306
By 2017. Musk's engineers will build a time machine.

item.title: MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM. IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS. IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN. IT'S A… — 19 replies — 32.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM. IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS. IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN. IT'S A…

Open thread • Replies: 19 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:07 UTC

OP

MARS MISSION IS A COMPLETE SCAM.

IT IS PHYSICALLY IMPOSSIBLE TO SUSTAIN HUMAN LIFE ON MARS.

IT WILL NEVER HAPPEN.

IT'S A CASH GRAB.

Early replies (first 3)

62358478
>>62358421
why does he need more cash if not to go to mars?

62358533
>>62358478
To be more rich

62358563
They have enough compute for really good deepfakes. There was never any intention to go. We learned from NASA that all you need is pretty pictures and th spacekeks will froth

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358665
>>62358478
For jews its a dick measuring contest.

62359904
Impossible? No. Difficult? Yes. Very, very difficult.

62360411
>>62358421

62360427
>>62358421
It's not just that the Mast mision is unviable, it is that they are telling you that a company that can't manage to make money on Earth is somehow going to make money on Mars kek.

62360573
>>62358421
More importantly in the context of /biz/, it isn't profitable

62360832
>>62358563
why compute and not computation

62360954
>>62360573
Correct. Not profitable in the lifetime of any living person. But someday maybe.

62361241
It'll happen but not for another 100 years at least, most especially at the snails pace tech has "advanced" since the new millennium.

62361299
>>62361241
Tech has advanced extremely fast, but all that advancement has happened on the digital side since that's where the money is.
"Let the goyim deal in the real world" and all that.

62361307
>>62358478
Fund his many other failing businesses

Mars is red, dry, Antarctica but hey at least it has extremely high radiation levels too, enough to give you cancer in weeks. Oh and long term low gravity effects that would cause god knows what health issues. Oh and the soil is toxic as well.


Mars colonization is a scam

62363330
>>62358421
I read that the atmosphere is so thin that it feels like a warm summer's day at your feet at 20°C but your head would freeze instantly at -150°C

62363343
Mars is stupid, cold and empty.
Lets find a similar Earth in the Andromeda Galaxy.

62363397
>>62363343
what if they have niggers too

62363420
Do people realise Mars' gravity is like 1/3 of Earth? It's closer to the Moon's gravity than to the Earth, you'd have to smash like 2 Mercury's worth of material into Mars to get Earth-like gravity. Anybody on Mars will eventually get fucked up from their bodies being in such a low-g environment not to mention what would happen to soft-boned babies.

62363426
>>62363397
Imagine geniuses building a device to replace space metrics to "travel", find another Earth like planet, it's teeming with life, esrablish a colony, and the smartest animal there is roughly half as smart as the clever mathematicians and astronauts who travelled. So instead of providing cuteness and pats, they wreck the colony and ask for resources because it's our fault they ate everything.

62363459
whites just like to go venture out

item.title: Anyone else here all in VT? I feel like my hair has finally stopped finally stopped falling out — 20 replies — 31.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
Anyone else here all in VT? I feel like my hair has finally stopped finally stopped falling out

Open thread • Replies: 20 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:04 UTC

OP

Anyone else here all in VT? I feel like my hair has finally stopped finally stopped falling out

Early replies (first 3)

62357349
I’m like 80% VTI/VTSAX/whatever Fidelity’s S&P 500 fund is. the rest is in Apple that I bought before I knew about index funds.

62357428
>>62357306
you know your bonds will go down in value if rates go up?

62357471
>>62357428
VT is a 100% equity fund that contains no bonds

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62357483
Nah...the only thin worse than vt is a target fund. You buy al th shitty stocks in the world but at least your passing on 3rd woels bonds i guess. Even schd beats vt

62357524
>>62357483
I don't think the U.S. will dominate forever. I can't predict the future either, so rather just own the entire haystack instead of waste my time searching for tiny bits of better gains.

62357531
>>62357306
Yea. I only gamble with chump change and profits go into my boring VT stack. All Hail Jack Bogle

62357560
>>62357524
yeah, invest in nifty 500 and CSI 300 instead of that piece shit and piss 500.

62357587
>>62357524
Thats fine, but there are better funds than just buying everything.

62357627
>>62357587
There's always going to be something better, but the best to me is whatever will keep from tinkering with it, no panic selling, never abandoning it if it underperforms, just set and forget while I live my life with the lowest stress possible.

62357735
>>62357306
Honestly man, the final whitepill is that if you have a good savings rate, 100% into VT, and actually live your life to the fullest instead of looking at charts, you'll have made it without much stress. I guess you won't find most VT investors in places like /biz/, they're just not looking at the charts.

62357837
>>62357735
I still find /biz/ interesting and may throw a hail marry at some 100x opportunity to throw back into VT, but haven't seen anything show up since meme season a couple years ago or stuff like Chainlink before that. I'm pretty sure most feel a major gap hence the userbase here is like 10% of what it used to be.

62360556
>>62357531
We wouldn't have this board without him. Low cost index funds and free trades are because of him. Vanguard drug all the other brokerages kicking and screaming into the reality of no fee trading.

RIP Jack John Bogle.

His idea was that bogleheads is better than trading without the skill or just leaving your money in a savings account. be happy with better than nothing and skill the part where you pay some shit talker salesman half your profits.

62360568
>>62360556
Skip instead of skill.

EDIT

62360692
The more people that buy it, the more exit liquidty for the jews

62360737
>>62357306
investing in shitty bank in Netherlands for the sake of diversification. just goyim things.

62361324
Yeah I have like >75k shares of vt and play league of legends all day. It’s a quiet life. Will I underperform voo? Maybe but I really don’t give a fuck anymore and the higher dividend prevents me from ever selling

62362673
>>62357306
I tried it once, it just made me more anxious because it felt like throwing money away as it was growing so slow. I just felt inflation creeping up and all the missed gains, it was not a fun time.

62363427
>>62362673
>Tried it once
you're supposed to just set and forget. If you max all the tax advantaged accounts you'd be up a ton every year plus the VT gains. Doesn't work if you have a shit job though, min 6 figures.

62363438
We need a VT general to rival the gay shitcoins

62363454
i have $2.0M in VTI between my 401k rollovers and txable account, and 100 eth. would like to move eth to vti before it pops. hope vti is correct

item.title: WTF IS GOING ON HERE????? — 18 replies — 28.5/hr
item.content:encoded:
WTF IS GOING ON HERE?????

Open thread • Replies: 18 • Updated: 2026-06-13 16:04 UTC

OP

Tesla is losing profit every year

Spacex has never made a profit.

Musk is worth 1 trillion

What the fuck is going on here?

Early replies (first 3)

62360949
SpaceX has access to space. No other company does. Tesla will be bought by SpaceX. SpaceX will be bought by the United States if it's not profitable

62360950
>>62360939
Failing up

62360957
Investors are trend whores first and foremost and all the shit he does is trendy
>electric cars
>low latency satellite internet
>robots
>rockets
>data centers
>ai
Maybe step aside and realize your world take that creates a hostile view of these things is wrong? People want this stuff and Elon provides it.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62360960
clown world

i bought because clown world

62360970
>>62360939
SPCX will be the most valuable company by marketcap by June 30th. Screencap this.

62360973
>>62360949
Correct.

Starlink is a global monopoly on worldwide internet access.

https://starlink.com/gb/map?srsltid=AfmBOoqlVTOgpN3ehfFOgyMLltD3ldYMI3VvILC7M6l5Cwbk1a3JiAlM

62361061
>>62360939
when did they replace the Elon android with a chink version?

62361070
I started praying to Elon in 2024 so he save my village and it work. All my village rich from AI now.

62361094
>>62360939
Stocks are more about feelings and vibes now a'days then they are about actual P&L. So it doesn't actually matter that both of Musk's companies are complete dog water as long as they seem "cool."

Kind of the same thing with cryptocurrencies. They have no actual intangible value, but people think they're part of the ingroup if they buy some.

62361118
>>62360939
>girls FTW!

62361128
>>62360939
Starlink is the answer.

62361157
>>62360973
>Starlink is a global monopoly on worldwide internet access.
>>62361128
we have satphones (+internet) for quite some time now (many decades) don't we.
are they all fumbling up the Laffer Curve and should lower price x100 but get x10,000 the customers, go to market area where spaceX is?

62361173
>>62360949
This

62361175
Starlink would be a great concept if it could operate autonomously. Imagine accepting crypto payments so that users anywhere could not only access the service but also browse the internet completely free from any local censorship or restrictions.
Unfortunately the whole thing relies on land infrastructure to connect the end user, so it still needs to comply with the laws of the location the user is at.

62361455
>>62360939
Pump and dump as one last big "fuck you" to brain dead investors that trusted large cap ETFs and tech-bro lemmings.

SpaceX by itself is a reasonably investable company, but the retarded structure and merges between xAI and X (formerly Twitter) are to let insiders rug pull on everyone that are either willing purchasers of the stocks or are forced to buy it with the blatantly jewish rule change to allow the company into the NASDAQ and S&P500.

Anyone sane should be selling their VOOs and Russell 1000. Literally anything else is a better investment right now: gold, crypto, mining, utilities, etc.

62361472
>>62360939
Why do you retards still think profit = share price?

62363450
>>62360973
>>62361128
Terrestrial mobile and fiber networks cover the vast bulk of the market by value

62363455
>>62361472
If the market is efficient, the share price should reflect the present value of expected future profits.

item.title: (srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb? — 21 replies — 22.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
(srs post) thoughts on my nostradamus'ing? Good? Meh? Dumb?

Open thread • Replies: 21 • Updated: 2026-06-13 15:46 UTC

OP

June 11: chop as market gets ready for pump on spacex trading day

June 12 - June 16: iran FUD is metabolized and market goes up on spacex


June 17 - June 22: FOMC on June 16 EOD causes fud, plus more iran fears

June 22 - Oct 10: NDQ floats upwards 40% while S&P chops and eventually goes up 10%

Oct 10: NDQ reaches a generational top - OpenAI IPOs - Fed announces more hikes due to AI labor inflation - republicans lose congress making trump a lame duck president = no more stimulus grift to pump markets
Oct 10, 2026 - ~Oct 10 2027: as a consequence of the lame duck presidency and the lack of stimulus and the selling of AI venture capital bags onto retail, S&P sells off 40% to round trip all the way back down to 5000

~Oct 10 2027: anthropic ipos

~Oct 10 2027 onwards: pretty much a new 20 year cycle begins and most likely the end of humanity as we birth sentience of machines.

Early replies (first 3)

62349969
you need to factor in the wc which is the biggest npc bread & circus event in modern history

62349972
>>62349960
i just matched up the timelines for 1973 and 2000 btw, that's where I'm getting the play by play for things. I reckon the trading AI's are probably averaging the two together given the macro environment

62349980
>>62349969
ah good point, ya so that might erase the june 17 - june 22 fud period and we go straight into june 22 - oct 10 period

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350015
>>62349960
Eh. I think they'll just turn shit up to 11 because Bank of America lost.

62352238
biz, so far my prediction is working, stop posting on 1pbtid pepe threads, and let me know if my predictions are shit or not

62353716
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's

62353764
>>62353716
It'll be a lost decade. It'll take 5-7 years for first new nuclear reactors to come online to unclog the the backlog of ai data centers that will be getting shit canned later this year or next after the crash.

Commodities will be the main play. World needs real resources to build infrastructure and robots. Shit doesn't mine itself out of the ground.

62353866
>>62353764
thanks, yea, seems to be that way. Right around 1973 market peak to 1975ish, gold picked up a 2x bid, so I imagine other commodities would follow suit

It's hard to imagine though, because I look around and I still to this day don't see labor inflation, yet everyone is speaking about how all this build out is happening. If it's happening, why doesn't it show up in labor? That's the first bottleneck that'll show up, no? It showed up in 08, are we saying all the mining and manufacturing just simply doesn't require that many humans? That seems a little bit farfetched to me

62355245
>>62349960
elf

62359675
snailposting this shit since you retards only want to respond to 1pbtid's x2

62359708
Sex with Frieren

62359749
>>62359675
what do you want to hear? the market will go up or go down. your doom pronouncement is stupid since people have been predicting the apocalypse for thousands of years and have always been wrong. also LLMs are gay and AI companies deserve to be buried in piracy and antitrust lawsuits by the next administration. I hope every AI ceo ends up in jail

62359780
>>62359708
Sex with this anon

62359931
>>62359749
fuck yourself, im gonna keep snailposting till you retards give me some concrete nostradamus dates and counter reasoning exactly like you retards do on the 1pbtid pepe threads

62359962
Line will continue up until 7/4/28
7*4 = 28 and they get the satisfaction of destroying a holiday. Will also hand everything to the dems in the next election.

62359991
>>62349960
This is now a Flielen thread

62359998
>>62359991

62361834
snailposting for continued feedback, you're not getting rid of me that easily

62361911
>>62349960
Woah wait a second Frieren, the FOMC is next week? Fucccccccckkkk I need to deleverage.
>FOMC -> NDQ goes up
Walk me through this part if they hike wouldn't that cause a dump or something not more pumping? So should I hold my bullish positions or not? Also I'm pretty sure both Anthropic and OpenAI ipo this year in that order.

62361941
>>62353866
this year I saw a lot of ads for oil related jobs masquerading aa youtube shorts
not sure if that counts

62363423
>>62361911
when trump said he'll be signing the agreement on sunday, the target rates dumped

https://www.cmegroup.com/markets/interest-rates/cme-fedwatch-tool.html

warsh is gonna come out next week and be dovish, but the market will act like its not simply because they're range trading, and they're gonna rebuy their positions.

62360066

62360120
>>62360040
yeah it's pathetic


i would pay to see some of these boyscouts give a live presentation in their own words about one of those infographics like the one in op.
the chainlink boyscout revels in the fact that their cult investment comes off as so high iq that no one can understand it, including themselves if they're being honest.

the position for them is essentially a vanity hold, where they can fantasize about the day they show the world their misunderstood, off the charts intelligence. the problem is of course, that day isn't real

their hubris has been quite costly over the years

62360137
>>62359988
>>62360040

62360230
>>62360137

we're doing just fine in the material realm without the assistance of your supposed "son of God" who was born in "immaculate conception" (lmao cope for Mary being a whore)

62360247
>>62355053
Three more years.... then my real life can begin

62360331
>>62360230
Im praying still

62360405
>>62360331
well stop

62360577
>>62355053
Yjk this nigger typed 2 more years but decided to throw an extra year in there because it would be too on the nose. Evil fucking nigger kek

62360584
>>62355583
>Flat feet bug gf poster is back
>Link down another -30% since
Sorry bro you better get used to the flapjack feets

62360663
>>62354976
did you take the vax?

62360802
>>62360066
What if link is actually worth something but you can't use it to buy bread. Like kinda like if you had an entire continent in your name but you still need the knowledge to extract resources, work with local governing bodies etc etc

Basically what if we have tokens but are unable to use them because we're goyim

62360825
>>62360137
>duuuuuude it's all a consssssssthhhhpirathy!!!

there he is everyone, our very own sleuth

excuse me, DETECTIVE sleuth

nothing, and i mean NOTHING, gets by this guy. he can sniff out colluding posts in seconds. this guy is GOOD, and i feel ashamed at ever trying to get one by him

afterall, everyone knows that there's nothing more important than guarding the reputation of a dead altcoin on a dead forum


well, you know what we say here...

THE Cuckolds of crypto

62360858
>>62360802
Well, then we're fucked either way.

62360859
>>62360825
>>62360405
Jesus loves you

62360865
>>62360859
religion is the opiate of the masses
or in this case, the stink marines

62360873
>>62360865

62360876
>>62360873
your "forgiveness" is rejected, stinkie
how does that make you feel

62361417
>>62360876
feels to me like you're gonna take my whole cock in your ass, and you're gonna like it.

62361431
>>62354976

This year was always the plan for chainlink

62361469
>>62360876
Lilla My, why do you have so much hatred for stinkies?

62361506
I thought green man sergay was going to make me rich...

62361627
>>62358895

62361699
>>62354943
7 years
7 dollars

62361781
>>62361773

62362456
it's very easy, either it works or I'll keep my stack as a lesson in humility

there is no scenario where I sell

62362461
>>62362456
cool beta attitude, stinker
just admit you lost the game of el lifio

62363300
>>62359501
>>62360137
>>62360331

62363331
>>62363300

jesus would rebuke all linkies
"i do not know you, stinkie"

62363473

Ad

62362844

62363356

item.title: Law of the jungle — 65 replies — 13.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Law of the jungle

Open thread • Replies: 65 • Updated: 2026-06-13 11:41 UTC

OP

>I am a million billion trillionair-
>BAM BAM BAM
>STAB STAB STAB
W-WHY AM I DYING I AM RICH AND HAVE MONEY ROCKETS CARS AIEEEEEE
P-PLEASE SIRE SAVE M-
>BAM BAM BAM
NOOOO WHY IS MONEY USELESS WHY WHY WHY WAAAAH PLEASE SIR
I WILL GIVE YOU EVERYTHIN-
>STAB HIS KIDS
AAAAAAH MY KIDDIES MY BLOODLINE WHY WHY
PLEASE SIR THE TRILLION MONEY
ACK

Early replies (first 3)

62359354
>>62359336
a mentally buck busted normie is going to say """the law""" grants the rich physical immunity.... and even if it doesn't, they have like, body guards who are omnipotent, omniscient, and omnipresent. you can't do ANYTHING to the rich!!!! it's literally impossible!!!!!

62359360
>>62359336
We live in capitalism and I'm protected by the pigs u need to learn how to move nigga

62359387
>nigger tier envy thread
>/biz/

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62361033
>>62360991
The most important social contract is the Ten Commandments.
It's the commies and anarchist left that does away with them.

62361047
>>62360991
> rich defenders
Wtf is that even? Since when do intelligence and/or success deserve to be seen as punishable negatives? What's there to defend about being rich? Are you insane?

62361048
>>62360896
generational wealth? you make $200k and a $2 million net worth. lmao.

62361065
>>62360991
literally just pay him to chill. its that easy

62361074
>>62359496
Those are airsoft shit.

62361079
>>62360200
>kekaroo
This gay aussie /pol/ faggot spams /biz/

62361121
get a trillion dollars and find out what happens
oh, nothing happens? ok

62361214
I know a god will hunting troll when I see one.

62361399
>>62359496
These are BB guns, brownoid.
Google what that is, since you don't know.

62361400
>>62359336
Based

62361423
>>62361048
2 mil in stocks/crypto not 2 mil net worth and unlike you I don't need to cry and piss my pants over rich people

62361436
kinda rude

62361646
>>62361423
plywood box equity doesn't count. and you aren't rich, because you work for a living. literally no one in the history of mankind has created generational worth from working. you need to own a business, which you don't.

62361649
>>62361646
if he has that much in stocks and crypto he probably owns a business.

all you have to do is buy and sell in a single fiscal year and you're technically in business.

62361653
>>62359336
Nigger, you will never do shit, you never have done shit. Your impotent crying school shooter fantasy will not improve you situation, while your mother is getting rutted by the tenth nigger of the day

62361673
>>62360694
kek poorfag and a stinkoid brownoid baggie. i am so much better than you its unreal

62361674
>>62361653
>nigger cuck bbc fantasy posting while defending trillionaire jews online
i am whiter than you

62361730
>>62360200
>>62361079
Interesting fact about "Kekaroo":

Not once in the history of the word "Kekaroo" did the person typing it actually laughed. It's the ultimate "I'm not mad, I'm laughing" internet argument seethecope word.

62361735
>>62361674
>i am whiter than you
>things white people don't say
there's no "whiter"
you either are white or you are not.

you are not

62361872
>>62359336
if you were rich you wouldn't live around the jungle.

62361876
>how do you respond without sounding mad?
BANG BANG BANG BANG

62361887
>>62359400

62362019

62362087
>>62361730

62362109

62362126
>>62359336

62362156
>>62359336

62362331
>>62359336

item.title: How will this affect South Koreas economy? — 32 replies — 12.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Open thread • Replies: 32 • Updated: 2026-06-13 14:11 UTC

OP

How will this affect South Koreas economy?

Should I go all-in on a SK index fund?

Early replies (first 3)

62350030
>>62349989
I like that he recognizes bald suffering, but the issue is mostly rampant feminism, so effectively, he is an enabler.

62350349
>>62349989
by shaving you no longer have to worry about styling your hair and it's a repellent for nasty and bad women

62350516
>>62350349
>just bend over and let the norwood reaper fuck you in the ass

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62350557
in the usa you should be able to pay for finasteride and minoxidil with an HSA. hair is protection from skin cancer.

62350562
>>62350349
You dont look like a 90s skinhead, you skinnyfat onions baldy.

62350565
>>62350557
Almost everywhere in the world generic finasteride options cost like $20-30 for an entire years supply.

Are the pharma companies scamming burgers by overcharging finasteride like they do with most other meds?

62350592
Baldy, baldy over there...

62350594
>>62350565
Except fin is a tranny drug poison. Post finasteride syndrome reddit has 15k unique weekly visits.

62350624
>>62350594
About 20% of men between 18-35 (who arent on fin) develop ED.

When people who do take fin develop ED, of course the drug that nukes users dicks (temporarily) 1% of the time is what they'll blame it on, while in reality it's something else entirely. I'd bet the number of finasteride users who develop permanent ED is pretty close to 20%, the same percentage as men who don't use it.

62350707
>>62349989
Just make it socially acceptable to wear hats everywhere. I am balding and I just wear a hat all the time and I was still able to get laid. Basically by the time I make it to the point the hat comes off it is too late and the sex happens anyway. I'd imagine this is what it is like when trans women trick straight men into bed with them.

62350754
>>62350707

62350920
>>62349989
attractive men = baby boom

62351695
mandatory plastic surgery
everyone looks like kpop
big orgy ensues

62351707
>>62350594
Finasteride has a half life of 6 hours and PFS is a fake condition invented by American grifters and bald cucks.

62351757
>>62349989
good work mr president, that outta improve the birth rate

62352443
>>62350565
20 a year? I pay 9 a month
t. eufag

62352757
>>62352443
I’m eu too and I get a 98-pack of 5mg for about €35. Split the 5mg into 4 pieces and you have 392 doses.

Never heard of anyone actually being prescribed individual 1mg tablets here. Pretty much all doctors prescribe the 5mg option and tell them to split it in 4

62352787
>>62350565
no, it is also cheap in the US. it’s just annoying that you can’t buy it with an HSA. it’s also annoying that you need a prescription. should be OTC.

62355508
>>62350624
>>62351707
Literally everyone develops watery semen on it, have fun taking that poison that will fuck you up after some years, instead of just getting a toupe.

62355814
>>62355508
I've taken it for 4 years and never experienced that. Looked it up now and in clinical trials it was a side-effect that hit about 3% of users, and which went back to normal when they discontinued usage.

Tens of millions of men are on finasteride and like 95% of guys who go on the internet to talk about it are doing so because they had negative effects from it and need advice. It's like going to a subreddit about sexual assault and assuming that just because 90% of the people posting there got raped means that 90% of people in real life have been raped as well, when that is obviously far from true

62355979
>>62350349
*repellent for all women

62356219
The mistake most men make when jumping on finasteride is not getting their testosterone and DHT levels checked first. Since using a drug that nukes your DHT while having low T and by extension low DHT is a recipe for disaster.

Like 20% of men between 18-35 have clinically low T (under 300ng/dl) that warrants medical treatment. Up to 30% are in the danger zone (under 350ng/dl) with mild symptoms of low T. Any doctor who prescribes finasteride to these people and nuking their DHT levels even further deserve to lose their license for malpractice

62360477
>>62349989
>free hair transplants
>free lefort / bimax surgery
>free limb lengthening
>free boobjobs
>free rhinoplasty
>free miscellaneous cosmetic surgery
Honestly not a bad solution to a dying fertility rate

62360611
>>62350624
tranny well poisoner
no, dht is an important hormone and you cant just block it because your reddit sisters say the science is safe
>its not temporary
i have not taken it but i know dudes who have gotten permanently nooked form fin

62360635
>>62350754
I actually had this once, stopped me at the door and asked me to take my beanie off
I do so and he goes "DONT WORRY MATE!!! DONT WORRY, IM BALD TOO ITS OKAY"

62360696
>>62349989
shocked he's not focused on the teenie weenie epidemic.

62360743
>>62350707
Not watery semen at all. My shit works normal but I have zero opportunity to use it because fin isn't a miracle drug. I'm bald af. Still taking fin+min and using spray paint and hair fibers to fill gaps.

Maybe if I bust like 9 times in a short period of time might make it watery. My libido is crazy high. My favorite thing about fin is it prevents prostate issues. You might also find you are attracted to nearly every woman you encounter. So maybe expect heavier post nut clarity.

62360773
>>62350349
I don't have the right head shape to do that. I'd look fucked up even more than I already do.

62360967
>>62355814
did it make ur head smaller?

62362891
>>62355814
You will be on that crap for decades so enjoy the few good years left and you will see.

62362975
>>62360967
Clearly it shrunk his brain

62363126
>>62360967
>>62362891
>>62362975
>it made my dick stop working so it has to happen to everyone else too. it’s not fucking fair!!

lmao, cope limpdick baldies

item.title: You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn — 78 replies — 12.4/hr
item.content:encoded:
You MUST post in this thread if you are waging rn

Open thread • Replies: 78 • Updated: 2026-06-13 10:24 UTC

OP

wage

Early replies (first 3)

62351330
I'm currently hiding in the bathroom

62351353
>>62351325
Im not desu

62351358
>>62351325
i hate being a wagie so fucking much

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62354681
I'm sitting on the toilet now while waging.

I always take multiple shits per day while being paid for it.

62354699
>>62354675
It’s such a joke. Trucking is a nightmare, no wonder jeets do it

62354715
>>62352824
I can't or I get in biiiiiiig trouble

62354716
>>62351325
I being paid to sleep in a hotel because tradies keep fucking up the infrastructure for our machine

62354761
>>62354699
Can't believe how many jets have jumped into the industry. I feel like the average normie isn't aware of their presence in that sector.

62354806
>>62354761
People have no idea how much danger they are in on the highway because of the shitjeets. It’s an existential threats to everybody

62354992
>>62354661
Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work

62355007
>>62354992
> Idk maybe I'm weak but I need to "decompress" after work
Same. It is so draining.

62356088
I have alot of down time at my workplace too
big issue for me is a 2 hour commute every day but I put up with it since my rent is cheap

62356156
>>62353364
Ash Grove. In that pic I was troubleshooting a truck loading issue. I discovered that our loading system was haunting loading due to some shutdown condition for a split second and tgen deciding to be happy again, most likely fluctuations in the scale wait from the truck or scale shaking while loading. The momentary blip breaks a logic latch forcing a retard of the loading process each time. Now I get to find out how to use timer off delays in unity pro and proworx32 to filter out that momentary blip. Every day is a new problem I have to figure out and I enjoy that.

62356177
>>62356156
I like how my phone knows that I call people retards on 4chan so often it autocorrect a typo of reload to retard

62356574
I'm shitting

62356712
I have an HR adult daycare job. I’m busy for maybe 40 hours a month. Most of my free time is spent shitposting here or reading the news. Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon.
>>62356088
I just got my MBA, mostly paid for by the job. I used pretty much all the downtime I typically had to do coursework at work. Shit was so cash.

62356735
I'm taking a break and sitting on a bench at the Bass Pro Shop museum in Dania Beach

62356749
I'm wfh, haven't done anything all afternoon except shitpost on /biz/ and do some laundry

62356784
I'm trying to hire a line cook but nobody wants to work. Need to find a make work adult daycare job.

62357448
>>62356712
How do you get a bullshit HR job if you are a dude? Are you brown?

62357470
>>62351325
I hope we all make it and never have to do this shit again

62357873
I'm at the airport lounge enjoying booze and snacks. Today is remote work day, just finished a teams call, now I just need to wrap up the week by spending about a minute to update one confluence item.

62358052
>>62351325
Nah I live in Europe so I'm done for the week. 100% WFH cybersecurity. Shit's so cash.

62358146
>>62357448
I’m White.
I got lucky and was hired by the HR department’s budget manager, who is also a white male.
My role is more that of an accountant than what you would think of as an HR role, but in the company registry I’m technically HR. My boss (that same budget manager) and I handle the HR department’s finances and try to keep the roasties on budget. They’re pretty deferential, and my boss is very hands on, so that doesn’t leave me with a lot to do beyond monthly reconciliation reports and paying some bills as they come up.
It’s a sweet gig, the only shit part is that it pays peanuts.

62358716
>>62356712
>Today the only thing I have going on is a 1 hour meeting in the afternoon
And they just canceled it, lel. My weekend is starting early.

62359402
>>62351325
I'm in class, but I'm not paying attention to it.

62359670
>>62351325
wrong image OP

WAGE

62360176
>>62351868
I want to visit Thailand.

62360948
>>62356735
Holy comfy

62360968
>>62354378
How?

62361451
I legitimately don't think about my job at work 80% of the time as I'm usually busy browsing /biz/, playing online Catan or talking to Claude while "working". The other 20% is me trying to figure out how I asking out my coworker so obviously likes me, but without the chance of having an awkward conversation with HR before my termination.

62362712
I worked on a quick Mars wargame scenario to de-rad today. It was nil casualties and the pursuit craft went kamikaze on a...pfft...thorium mine mistaking it for Hutchie HQ...idiot.

62362766
>>62354589
third wordie here, I'm teaching kids english online, shitpay but at the least I get to slack off behind the screen if the kid is doing ok. Had been trying to find better deals but my brain is currently fried from gaming so doing this to stall out my situation in the meantime.

item.title: SPCX is the new bitcoin. — 12 replies — 11.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Open thread • Replies: 12 • Updated: 2026-06-13 15:38 UTC

OP

SPCX is the new bitcoin.

Early replies (first 3)

62362526
>>62362506
You honestly might be right

62362529
>>62362506
So it's finna crash hard?

62362543
Nah nah nah, the tam is saying each share is worth $22,000. We are in chipotle hours now!

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62362551
I'll give you my DD.

I was watching Bloomberg this entire week. Every single day they avoided talking about SPCX. Everytime it was brought up, they said it was worth nothing.

Even early in the morning the day of the IPO, they kind of didn't talk about it until open. They didn't want to show Elon playing rocket man and so on. I kept watching Bloomberg and then something weird started happening. The hosts started getting really angry and salty. They kept bring up blue origin as a threat if they ever IPO'd and wanted to not talk about spacex anymore.

I continued to watch and eventually interviews started coming in where boombers were angry that they didn't get shares allocated to them and this Business Professor at the duke university got red in the face and said he was going to tell his university MBA students about this sham of an IPO. He rose up 1 finger and said "that's all I got, 1 share!"

That's when I knew this was the new bitcoin. It is born out of butthurt.

62362914
Mark my words. SPCX is going to $80k.

62362917
They hold bitcoin on the balance sheet

62362933
>>62362506
yep, -70% is programmed

62362934
>>62362506
No it's not

62363082
>>62362914
>SpaceX will be worth $100 Trillion dollars
full psychosis in effect

62363210
>>62363082
Saaar sonething sinething infinity liquidity benchid xerp 4774737266495577261739$ tumor weeks!

62363215
>>62362551
kek ok I'm on board but what about anthropic and other ipos

62363402
>>62363082
>>62363210
What a couple of prats!

item.title: The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. — 43 replies — 8.7/hr
item.content:encoded:
The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants.

Open thread • Replies: 43 • Updated: 2026-06-13 11:45 UTC

OP

The person who owns this isn't "on vacation." He does whatever he wants whenever he wants. He's upper class. And has 3 passports and owns properties and businesses (wealth) around the world. He isn't waiting on a paycheck to drive down to Costco.
Hint: it's a Rothschild property.
Making even a million bucks a year writing code for Elon or Bezos or Brin does NOT put you in the upper class. At all.
I am European and only we have class. We invented class.

Early replies (first 3)

62356062
>>62356048
Before AI, you could write code for ten years and then buy this.

62356086
Got so mad in the other thread you thought you would start a new thread about it LMAO

62356191
this is that same polish guy who was seething in the other thread about class or whatever

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62358606
>>62356048
>AAAACKKKKKKK

62358695
>>62356048
That place looks like a demonic kike festival. Hard pass. Heres a life tip. Don't get caught up with material things or worldly possessions. It looks nice but the people who live such extravagant lives are always empty morally corrupt husks and their children are absolute degenerates. Instead of aspiring to live like rich jews. Aspire spiritual wealth. Talk to God. Meditate on the words of Jesus Christ. Walk on the narrow road that leads to life and do not be mislead by the idolatrous jewish elites that reject the Living God and the Truth

62358945
>>62358433
I need to pay the bills
>>62358695
Based. You are not far from the Kingdom of God, but don't kid yourself, there's still a lot to do

62358989
>>62358606
>"Death is the great equalize...-ACCKKK!!!"

62359005
>>62358989

62359167
>>62356048
>dickriding gloating by association
>this lifestyle was real in my mind
Cool story, op
Lunch is over, time to clock back in

62359177
>>62358695
Based

62359194
>>62358695
>Jesus Christ
>demonic kike

62359210
>>62358989
It still is, but you can live your life like a beta peasant trying to extend your life seeking a philosopher stone (if only to perpetuate your suffering) or live like a chad pharaoh accepting death and building his own sarcophagus and tomb during his lifetime. Two different games, yet the same ending

62359212
>>62358695
Where the fuck is Christ? Why has he foresaken me? Is he even fucking real?

62359254
>>62358989
You don't even know the name of the guy who built those.

62359275
>>62356048
yeah and he is never happy and is made of the same carbon we all are. class is fake. wealth is fake. you can be happy with nothing easier than being happy with everything.

62359283
>>62359212
the real god made himself known to man a lot longer than 2000 years ago. it was pre history, so of course we know nothing about it. abrahamic tradition is nonsense.

62359313
>>62356048
Damn I triggered this barely literate 2nd generation immigrant to America (note I never called him an American - he isn't) so bad. Damn, I am almost sorry since it's Friday but not sorry since you're brown.

62359371
>>62356048
I'm sure his life sucks everyone has a reason to cry about shit no amount of stucco will save your body from pain suffering and misery it is inescapable

62359452
>>62358695
God bless you please pray for me.

62359520
>class is following as many rules as possible while frowning and spending lots of money
Wow sounds like a good time sign me up

62359522
>>62358695
Oh please spare me the weak cope philosophy where Jesus christ literally gets abandoned by God when he needs him the most on the cross so there is no guarantee. God is a child's fantasy of cohesion that the world is simple and suffering has meaning, you think someone is watching you at all times and ur not smart enough to realize that emptiness is structural and is born out of language and it's a insurmountable gap that you can only move through and not ever reach. When u move towards it without expecting anything..that is when u can finally act finally be free from god at least,.to relate to the symbolic world of language differently in a way where u can actually do what you want.

62359740
>>62359522
You’re a fag or a drug or porn addict. Maybe all three. Fuck off retard.

62360058
>>62359212
>>62359283
"The fool says in his heart that there is no God"

Maybe try turning away your sinful ways, repenting and searching scripture instead of looking for handouts. Or you could just live out your days as a hard hearted rancid transgressor and go to the grave an unbelieving fool. Nothing anyone says can change your blackened heart. The Holy Spirit does that work.

62360102
>>62360058
why is a 24 year old mulatto born again christian zoomer trying to give me life advice?

62360116
>>62359522
you have free will to do what you want. But be warned that you lash out at the unblemished Lamb of God. The Alpha and the Omega who healed every infirmity and cast out demons from the land. The Good Shepherd who has defeated and and is Seated at the right hand of Power. The sad thing about people like you is that you are so fallen and your nature is so corrupt that you cannot even hear the Truth if it was read to you. Even the demons themselves that Jesus drove out of people KNEW that he was the Son of the Most High. Repent brother. The Kingdom of Heaven is at hand. We know not the day nor the hour of His return

62360814
>>62359005
>these death monuments will be renouned and respected and not some dinky little tourist hotsp-ACK

62361163
>>62357608
Why do people hate on these posts instead of try to figure out ways to improve their quality of life to have similar?

That's the whole point of this board. Instead of hating on people showing off their wealth figure out how to get it too.

I thought it was only like this on hip hop forums. Everyone hat s on each other and then refuse all ways of getting to it.

62361183
>>62359210
Or stop all or nothing thinking and find a way to live well so you can have wealth and not have to compromise yourself in any way?

62362527
>>62356048
Why would I want such a monstrosity.

62362541
>>62360116
>unfathomably based life speaking minister of /biz
I will miss you when the tranny kike mods ban you for speaking truth, but I know you will bear your cross with dignity.

62362654
>>62356048
why would you even want this? what are you going to use it for? throw parties 24/7 for people you fucking hate? If you live their 24/7 the nice view and garden just become mundane like everything else only now you have a lot less money

62362675
>>62362527
You’re not European, you would not get it.

62362885
>>62358695
>stay poor and let the Jews control wealth and power like a good goy
